settings.question: Which heat pump suits my home?
settings.answer: It depends on your home's size, existing heating system and budget. Ground source and air-to-water suit homes with a water-based system; air-to-air suits direct electric heating. We offer a free needs analysis.

settings.question: How much can I save with a heat pump?
settings.answer: A house in the Stockholm area typically saves 40–70 % of heating costs. Use our savings calculator for a quick estimate.

settings.question: Do I get a ROT deduction on heat pump installation?
settings.answer: Yes, you get a 30 % ROT deduction on the labour cost, up to 50,000 kr per person per year. We deduct it directly from your invoice.

settings.question: How long does an installation take?
settings.answer: Air-to-air is usually installed in one day. Air-to-water and ground source take 2–5 days, plus drilling time for ground source.

settings.question: What warranty does VARMA Energy provide?
settings.answer: We provide a five-year installation guarantee and follow the manufacturer's product warranty, typically 5–10 years.

settings.question: What does ground source heating cost in Stockholm?
settings.answer: A complete ground source installation for a house in Stockholm typically costs between 180,000 and 260,000 kr incl. drilling, before ROT deduction.

settings.question: Which heat pump is cheapest to run?
settings.answer: Ground source has the lowest running cost over the year as it has the highest and most consistent efficiency across seasons.

settings.question: How long does a heat pump last?
settings.answer: A modern heat pump lasts 15–20 years. The compressor is the most stressed component and is sometimes replaced after 12–15 years.

settings.question: Does the heat pump disturb the neighbours?
settings.answer: A modern air-to-water or air-to-air outdoor unit runs at around 45–55 dB at 1 metre. Ground source is completely silent outdoors.

settings.question: Can I combine a heat pump with solar panels?
settings.answer: Yes. Solar panels reduce the electricity purchase for the heat pump and significantly improve the overall economics.

settings.question: What is the best heat pump for a housing association?
settings.answer: Ground source and exhaust air recovery are most common in multi-family buildings. We always conduct a feasibility study before recommending a solution.

settings.question: What does an air-to-air heat pump installation cost?
settings.answer: Typically 18,000–35,000 kr incl. installation, depending on the model and property conditions. ROT deduction is applied to the labour cost.

settings.question: Do I need planning permission for an air-to-air heat pump?
settings.answer: No, planning permission is not normally required in the Stockholm area. If you live in a housing association, you need board approval.

settings.question: Is air-to-water as good as ground source?
settings.answer: Ground source has higher efficiency over the year, but air-to-water is cheaper to invest in and a good choice when drilling is not possible or practical.

settings.question: How deep is a ground source borehole drilled?
settings.answer: Typically 150–250 metres for a house in Stockholm. We size the system based on the home's energy needs.

settings.question: Is a permit required for ground source in Stockholm?
settings.answer: Yes, the municipality must approve the drilling. We handle the application as part of the installation.

settings.question: Does exhaust air work in my older home?
settings.answer: Exhaust air pumps require mechanical exhaust ventilation. We carry out a site visit and assess whether it works.

settings.heading: Frequently asked questions about heat pumps
settings.description: Short, straight answers to what Stockholm homeowners ask us most. Can't find your answer – call us or send a quote request.

settings.heading: Börja spara redan i vår
settings.description: Installationer bokas nu in för maj. Boka ett kostnadsfritt hembesök idag och få ett fast pris inom 48 timmar.
